unitTitle: Language: Skill, Tool, Form, and Function

lessons.title: Language: Skill, Tool, Form, and Function
lessons.titleMy: ဘာသာစကား - အရည်အချင်း၊ ကိရိယာ၊ ပုံစံ၊ နှင့် လုပ်ဆောင်ချက်
lessons.intro: Explore the fundamental aspects of language as a skill, tool, form, and function in our daily communication.
lessons.introMy: ဘာသာစကားကို အရည်အချင်း၊ ကိရိယာ၊ ပုံစံ နဲ့ လုပ်ဆောင်ချက်တွေအနေနဲ့ သဘောတရားများကို လေ့လာပါ။

questions.question: What is language?
questions.suggested_answer: Language is a skill, a tool for communication, a form of verbal and non-verbal expression, and a means to perform social functions.

questions.question: What do we use it for?
questions.suggested_answer: We use it to communicate with others, share information, express our feelings, ask questions, and perform daily social tasks.

questions.question: What does language do for us?
questions.suggested_answer: Language helps us connect with people, reflects and preserves our culture, passes traditions to the next generation, and defines our social identity.

questions.question: What would our life be like without language?
questions.suggested_answer: Without language, our life would be incredibly difficult because we would not be able to share complex ideas, express thoughts clearly, or build organized societies.

bonusQuestions.question: Which skill do babies develop first?

lessons.title: Countries, Nationalities and Languages
lessons.intro: Learn the names of countries, their nationalities, languages, and related adjectives.

questions.question: What is the nationality of someone from France?
What language is spoken in Japan?
What is the adjective for Italy?

bonusQuestions.question: What is the nationality for Myanmar?
Which country is also called 'The UK'?

lessons.title: Nouns in Apposition
lessons.titleMy: အပြန်အလှန်အဆိုပါ နာမ်များ
lessons.intro: Master the concept of nouns in apposition and how to identify them in sentences.
lessons.introMy: အပြန်အလှန်အဆိုပါ နာမ်များ၏ သဘောတရားကို နားလည်ပြီး စာတွင် သိရှိနိုင်ရန် လေ့ကျင့်ပါ။

questions.question: What is the noun in apposition in: 'Bagan, an ancient capital of Myanmar, has many pagodas'?
Identify the apposition in: 'Mt. Everest, the highest peak in the world, is in Nepal.'
questions.answer: the highest peak in the world

questions.question: What does 'noun in apposition' mean?
questions.answer: A noun or noun phrase placed next to another noun to explain or identify it.

questions.question: What is the apposition phrase in: 'The Nile, the longest river in the world, is in northeastern Africa'?
questions.answer: the longest river in the world

bonusQuestions.question: Find the noun in apposition: 'My son, a musician, lives with me.'
How many appositions are in: 'Judo, a Japanese martial art, originated from jujitsu, a samurai fighting technique'?

sections.topic: Language: Skill, Tool, Form, and Function

pre_reading.question: What is language?
pre_reading.suggested_answer: Language is a skill, a tool for communication, a form of verbal and non-verbal expression, and a means to perform social functions.

pre_reading.question: What do we use it for?
pre_reading.suggested_answer: We use it to communicate with others, share information, express our feelings, ask questions, and perform daily social tasks.

pre_reading.question: What does language do for us?
pre_reading.suggested_answer: Language helps us connect with people, reflects and preserves our culture, passes traditions to the next generation, and defines our social identity.

pre_reading.question: What would our life be like without language?
pre_reading.suggested_answer: Without language, our life would be incredibly difficult because we would not be able to share complex ideas, express thoughts clearly, or build organized societies.

paragraphs.english_text: The question, "What is language?" can have several different answers, and all of them may be acceptable. For our present purpose, however, let us look at language as a skill, as a tool for communication, as a form of communication, and as a means to perform many social functions.
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: ဘာသာစကားဆိုတာ ဘာလဲဆိုတဲ့ မေးခွန်းအတွက် လက်ခံနိုင်တဲ့ အဖြေများစွာ ရှိပါတယ်။ သို့သော် ဒီခါမှာ ဘာသာစကားကို အရည်အချင်း၊ ဆက်သွယ်ရေးအတွက် ကိရိယာ၊ ဆက်သွယ်ရေးပုံစံ နှင့် လူမှုဘဝ လုပ်ဆောင်ချက်များ ကျင့်သုံးရန် အခန်းကဏ္ဍအဖြစ် ထည့်သွင်းစဉ်းစားကြည့်ပါ။

paragraphs.english_text: First, language is a set of skills: listening, speaking, reading, and writing. They are often known as the four language skills. Of these four skills, the listening skill is the first to be developed by a baby.
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: ပထမအချက်အနေနဲ့ ဘာသာစကားဟာ နားထောင်ခြင်း၊ ပြောဆိုခြင်း၊ ဖတ်ခြင်း နှင့် ရေးခြင်းတွေအပါအဝင် စွမ်းရည်များ အစုအဖွဲ့တစ်ခု ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။ ဒီ အရည်စွမ်းရည်လေးခုကို ဘာသာစကား စွမ်းရည်လေးခု ဟုခေါ်သည်။ ဒီလေးခုထဲမှ နားထောင်ခြင်းစွမ်းရည်ကို ကလေးများက အကျင့်အရည် အရင်ဆုံး ပြုလုပ်လာခြင်းဖြစ်ပါတယ်။

paragraphs.english_text: Here, we should note that listening and speaking work together as a pair of skills, while reading and writing work together as another pair. We should note further that speaking and writing are productive skills, while listening and reading are receptive skills.
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: မှတ်သားရမှာက နားထောင်ခြင်းနဲ့ ပြောဆိုခြင်းက တွဲလျက် စွမ်းရည်မျိုးတစ်မျိုး အဖြစ် လုပ်ဆောင်ခြင်းအနည်းငယ်တစ်ခုခြင်းဖြစ်ပါတယ်။ ဖတ်ခြင်းနဲ့ ရေးခြင်းတွေကလည်း တစ်ခြား တွဲလျက် စွမ်းရည်များ အဖြစ် လုပ်ဆောင်ခြင်းများ ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။ နောက်ထပ် သတိပြုရမှာကတော့ ပြောဆိုခြင်းနဲ့ ရေးခြင်းတွေဟာ ဖန်တီးမှု စွမ်းရည်များ ဖြစ်တဲ့ အတွက် နားထောင်ခြင်းနဲ့ ဖတ်ခြင်းတွေကတော့ လက်ခံမှု စွမ်းရည်များ ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။

paragraphs.english_text: Second, language is a tool for communication. This means that we use language to speak to someone, or to write to someone. To speak, we use vocal symbols; to write, we use written symbols.
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: ဒုတိယအချက်ကတော့ ဘာသာစကားဟာ ဆက်သွယ်ရေးအတွက် ကိရိယာတစ်ခု ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။ ဒါက ငါတို့ ဘာသာစကားကို တစ်စုံတစ်ယောက်ထံ ပြောဆိုရန် သို့မဟုတ် စာဖြင့် ရေးတိုက်ရန် အသုံးပြုခြင်းဖြစ်ပါတယ်။ ပြောဆိုရန် ငါတို့ အသံအသုံးပြုခြင်းများ အသုံးပြုပါတယ်။ စာဖြင့်ရေးရန်တော့ စာသားအသုံးပြုမှုများ အသုံးပြုပါတယ်။

paragraphs.english_text: Third, communication may be in one of two forms: verbal communication, and non-verbal communication. In verbal communication, we use words and sentences. They may be spoken or written.
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: တတိယအချက်အနေနဲ့ ဆက်သွယ်ရေးမှာ ပုံသဏ္ဌာန်နှစ်မျိုး ရှိပါတယ်။ စကားအသုံးပြုခြင်းဆက်သွယ်ရေး နှင့် စကားမဆုံးလုံးဆက်သွယ်ရေး ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။ စကားအသုံးပြုခြင်းဆက်သွယ်ရေးတွင် ငါတို့ စကားလုံး နှင့် စာကြောင်းများ အသုံးပြုပါတယ်။ ၎င်းတို့သည် ပြောဆိုရန် သို့မဟုတ် ရေးတိုက်ရန် ဖြစ်နိုင်ပါတယ်။

paragraphs.english_text: Fourth, we use language to help us perform many social functions, which are very much part of our day-to-day life. This means that we use language: to greet or to bid goodbye, to ask questions, to give orders, to thank someone, to apologize, to request something, to offer, to refuse, to invite, to suggest, and to warn.
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: စတုတ္ထအချက်ကတော့ ဘာသာစကားကို နေ့စဉ်လူမှုဘဝ လုပ်ဆောင်ချက်များ ကျင့်သုံးရန် အသုံးပြုပါတယ်။ ဆိုလိုရင်း ငါတို့ အလျှောက်လည်း အသုံးပြုခြင်း၊ ကွင်းဆင်းခြင်း၊ မေးခြင်း၊ အမိန့်စာ ထုတ်ခြင်း၊ ကျေးဇူးတင်ခြင်း၊ တောင်းပန်ခြင်း၊ တောင်းခံခြင်း၊ အဆိုပြုခြင်း၊ ငြင်းပယ်ခြင်း၊ ဖိတ်ခေါ်ခြင်း၊ အကြံပြုခြင်း နှင့် သတိပေးခြင်းတွေအတွက် ဘာသာစကားကို အသုံးပြုပါတယ်။

paragraphs.english_text: In addition, language reflects culture, preserves culture, passes on culture from one generation to the next, preserves nationality, and also identifies social class.
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: ဒါ့အပြင် ဘာသာစကားဟာ ယဉ်ကျေးမှုကို ထင်ဟပ်စေတယ်။ ယဉ်ကျေးမှုကို ကြေးမုံကျင့်သုံးစေတယ်။ ယဉ်ကျေးမှုကို လူမှုတစ်မျိုးမှ တစ်မျိုးသို့ ကျေးဇူးတင်သွားစေတယ်။ နိုင်ငံသားခွဲခြားမှုကို ကြေးမုံကျင့်သုံးစေတယ်။ လူမှုအဆင့်အတန်းကို နားလည်စေတယ်။

part_A.instructions: Complete each sentence below with a word or phrase from the reading passage.

exercises.text: The four language skills are: ___________.
The first language skill to develop is ___________.
A baby begins to speak at the age of ___________.
exercises.answer: about one and a half years or so

exercises.text: Listening and ___________ work together as a pair of skills.
The other pair of skills includes ___________.
Speaking and writing are ___________ skills.
Language is also known as a ___________ for communication.
The two forms of communication are ___________ and ___________.
We use ___________ when we speak to help the listener understand better.
We use ___________ when we write to help the reader understand better.

part_B.instructions: Give a short answer to each question below.

exercises.question: When does a child begin to read and write?
exercises.answer: Only when the child goes to school.

exercises.question: What are the productive skills of language?
What are the receptive skills of language?
Why do we use gestures when we speak?
exercises.answer: To help the listener understand us better.

exercises.question: How do we help the reader understand what we write better?
exercises.answer: By using graphics such as pictures, photographs, charts, and tables.

exercises.question: What are the two forms of communication?
Do you want to learn any other foreign language apart from English? Why?
exercises.answer: Sample answer: Yes, I want to learn Japanese because I want to understand Japanese culture and communicate with Japanese people.

exercises.question: Which language skill is the most difficult for you to learn? Why?
exercises.answer: Sample answer: Writing is the most difficult skill for me because it requires more thought and careful grammar usage.

part_C.instructions: Write the function of each utterance within the brackets given.

exercises.utterance: May I use the phone, please?
It's very kind of you to help me.
Could you please scan the photo for me?
Go along this street and turn right.
exercises.function: directing someone how to get to a certain place
responding to words of thanks and gratitude

exercises.utterance: I don't quite agree with what you said.
Make three copies of this article.

part_A.instructions: Complete the table. Australia and China are done for you as examples.

part_B.instructions: Rewrite each sentence below, filling the blanks with appropriate words from the table in Exercise A.

exercises.text: Because I speak ___________ fluently, I did not have much trouble in France.
Those tourists are from Italy and they speak only ___________ and do not understand a word of English.
I wish to visit Vietnam but I do not speak a word of ___________.
Since you speak ___________ quite well, you should have no problem studying in the USA.
I plan to work in Korea, so I'm learning ___________ at the Yangon University of Foreign Languages.
A kimono is a traditional ___________ garment.
William Shakespeare is a very widely known ___________ playwright and poet.
I have never been to Laos and I have never tried ___________ food.
He grew up in Germany and he speaks ___________ fluently.
I like ___________ food though I don't know how to use the chopstick.

part_A.instructions: Spot the nouns in apposition in the following sentences and underline them.

exercises.text: There are many pagodas in Bagan, an ancient capital of Myanmar.
The ostrich, a kind of flightless bird, is found only in Africa.
My son, a musician, earns little and lives with me.
Loch Ness, a large mountain lake, is in Scotland.
Mt. Everest, the highest peak in the world, is in Nepal.
apposition_phrases: the highest peak in the world

exercises.text: I'd love to climb Kilimanjaro, the highest mountain in Tanzania.
Brussels sprout, a green vegetable like a tiny cabbage, is quite delicious to eat.
apposition_phrases: a green vegetable like a tiny cabbage

exercises.text: The Nile, the longest river in the world, is in northeastern Africa.
apposition_phrases: the longest river in the world

exercises.text: Judo, a Japanese martial art, originated from jujitsu, a samurai unarmed fighting technique.
